arts.ttl: Chiellini represents Juventus at UEFA lunch with PSV officials
arts.sum: Giorgio Chiellini made his first public appearance as a Juventus executive, attending the traditional UEFA lunch hours before the Champions League match against PSV Eindhoven. The former Juventus captain and defender, who recently rejoined the club in a management role, represented the Italian giants alongside executive Francesco Calvo at the “Del Cambio” restaurant in Turin’s Piazza Carignano. Chiellini, who assumed the position of Head of Football Institutional Relations just yesterday, is working closely with CEO Maurizio Scanavino and President Gianluca Ferrero. His new role involves representing the club in relations with national and international football institutions. arts.ttl: Seedorf: “Real Madrid are favourites, but Inter, Milan and Juve can compete with the best”
arts.sum: The Champions League legend Clarence Seedorf shared his thoughts on the new format and top contenders for this season’s competition in an interview with La Gazzetta dello Sport. “I’m curious about the new format. I liked the previous one, as the anticipation for big matches was part of the spectacle. But let’s give the new format time to show how it works,” Seedorf said. When asked about the favorites, he stated: “History tells us that Real Madrid is the team to beat, but many can compete. arts.ttl: Why isn’t Hirving Lozano playing for PSV against Juventus in Champions League game 1?
arts.sum: Hirving ‘Chucky’ Lozano will miss PSV Eindhoven’s UEFA Champions League opener against Juventus today, as the winger recovers from an injury suffered earlier this month. His most recent strike, in a 3-0 win over Go Ahead Eagles on September 1 - was his 50th for PSV, leading the club to pay tribute to the feat at last weekend’s home league match against NEC.
